entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop species studied for its response to water deficit during panicle development.

entities.name: WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: A condition of limited water availability during the panicle development stage.

entities.name: PANICLE DEVELOPMENT STAGE
entities.description: The specific growth phase of the crop including panicle initiation and flowering.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The measure of grain production from the main shoot and tillers.

entities.name: MAIN SHOOT PANICLE
entities.description: The primary reproductive structure on the main stem of the plant.

entities.name: TILLERS
entities.description: Secondary shoots that can produce grain and compensate for main shoot yield loss.

entities.name: MAIN SHOOT REMOVAL
entities.description: The experimental method of removing the main shoot at panicle initiation or flowering stages.

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The condition of providing sufficient water to the plants.

entities.name: NUMBER OF PRODUCTIVE TILLERS
entities.description: The count of tillers that successfully produce grain.

entities.name: NUMBER OF GRAINS PER PANICLE
entities.description: The count of grains produced on a single panicle.

entities.name: YIELD COMPENSATION
entities.description: The biological mechanism where tillers increase yield to offset losses in the main shoot.

entities.name: NUMBER OF GRAINS ON TILLERS
entities.description: The total count of grains produced by the tillers.

reduces the grain yield of the main shoot panicle
compensates for the loss of grain yield in the main shoot
causes loss of grain yield in the main shoot

is the stage during which water deficit occurs
